title: Permutation-Invariant Field-State Model
summary: Pit calls are not purely driver-local; they depend on the whole race-lap state.
rationale: Pit calls are not purely driver-local; they depend on the whole race-lap state. Deep Sets / Set Transformer style models are designed for permutation-invariant set inputs, which fits the set of cars present on a given lap better than hand-built race-lap aggregates.
implementation_hint: Build one sample per driver-lap, but encode all cars from the same Year-Race-Lap as a set. Concatenate the focal driver embedding with a pooled field embedding and predict PitNextLap. Use only same-lap information and OOF predictions as an ensemble member beside XGB/CatBoost.
expected_effect: Could capture higher-order interaction patterns missed by current mean/std/rank features and add strong blend diversity.
risk: More engineering and memory cost; if the field-state tensor is built carelessly it can leak fold information or overfit to race identity.
